title: Proposal title
text:

# load the dataset
penguins <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-04-15/penguins.csv')

title: Proposal title
section: A brief description of your dataset including its provenance, dimensions, etc. as well as the reason why you chose this dataset.
text: A brief description of your dataset including its provenance, dimensions, etc. as well as the reason why you chose this dataset.

title: Proposal title
section: Make sure to load the data and use inline code for some of this information.
text:
Make sure to load the data and use inline code for some of this information.
The Palmer Penguins dataset was first created in TidyTuesday in July 2020 and was last updated in 2025. The dataset has 8 columns/variables - Species, Island, Bill length, Bill depth, Flipper length, Body mass, Sex, and Year. There are 344 observations/rows of data on the various species of penguins. The species included in this dataset includes 3 types of penguins - Adelie, Gentoo, and Chinstrap. The dataset also focuses on 3 islands - Torgersen, Biscoe, and Dream. I chose this dataset as I love penguins and I would love to see how different factors can affect the different characteristics in penguins. It would also be interesting to see how the body mass of penguins change over the years through evolution.

title: Proposal title
text:
Questions

How does the island where the penguin resides impact their bill length?
How has the body mass of the Adelie, Gentoo, and Chinstrap penguins changed between 2007 and 2009?

title: Proposal title
text:
Analysis plan

A plan for answering each of the questions including the variables involved, variables to be created (if any), external data to be merged in (if any).

Question 1 plan
Variables involved:
- Island the penguin resides
- Species of penguins
- Bill length
Plan:

Create side-by-side box plots to compare bill lengths of penguin species and their bill length

Box plot variables:
x-axis = Species
y-axis = Bill length
fill = Island
Create a violin plot to compare the distribution of bill lengths for each penguin species
x-axis = Species
y-axis = Bill length(mm)
fill = Island
Question 2 plan
Variables involved:
- Body mass of penguins
- Penguin species
- Year
Plan:
Create a time series plot to compare the body mass of penguins for each species over the years
Time series plot variables:
x-axis = Year
y-axis = Body mass of penguins
fill = Penguin species

title: Project title
text:
Math Expressions
You can write LaTeX math expressions inside a pair of dollar signs, e.g. $\alpha+\beta$ renders \(\alpha + \beta\). You can use the display style with double dollar signs:
$$\bar{X}=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^nX_i$$
\[\bar{X}=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^nX_i\]
Limitations:

The source code of a LaTeX math expression must be in one line, unless it is inside a pair of double dollar signs, in which case the starting $$ must appear in the very beginning of a line, followed immediately by a non-space character, and the ending $$ must be at the end of a line, led by a non-space character;
There should not be spaces after the opening $ or before the closing $.

title: Project title
text:
Feeling adventurous?

You are welcomed to use the default styling of the slides. In fact, that’s what I expect majority of you will do. You will differentiate yourself with the content of your presentation.
But some of you might want to play around with slide styling. Some solutions for this can be found at https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs.
